research_brief.title: AI4S CNS Challenge Task 4: Neural Operator PDE Agent
research_brief.question: Build an autonomous scientific agent that can independently complete the PDE neural-operator research loop for the AI4S CNS Challenge Task 4, including literature understanding, bottleneck diagnosis, method innovation, code evolution, experiment execution, physical validation, and report generation.
research_brief.background: The challenge targets PDE neural operators as a scientific-agent problem rather than a static model-submission problem. The system should autonomously study PDEBench and representative neural-operator methods such as DeepONet, FNO, PI-DeepONet, PINN, and related physics-aware operator-learning approaches; identify weaknesses in existing baselines; acquire required repositories, datasets, and checkpoints; and iteratively improve methods through executable scientific workflows. The competition emphasizes autonomous research capability under CNS-style scientific standards, with particular focus on PDEBench 1D tasks where physical information must participate in training or inference.
objectives: Autonomously retrieve and synthesize literature on PDEBench, neural operators, physics-informed operator learning, and related PDE scientific ML methods.
Autonomously discover, download, inspect, and bootstrap the repositories, datasets, checkpoints, and documentation needed to run realistic PDE neural-operator experiments.
Diagnose bottlenecks in PDEBench 1D baselines for short-window prediction, especially around physical consistency, parameter sensitivity, and method-level limitations beyond local tuning.
Generate and test literature-grounded hypotheses that introduce scientifically meaningful method innovations rather than only hyperparameter optimization.
Implement code changes, run experiments on the target tasks, analyze logs and metrics, and maintain interpretable research memory and reports.
constraints: The target tasks are 1D Burgers short-window prediction and 1D Diffusion-Reaction short-window prediction.
Short-term predictive accuracy is the primary task metric.
Physical information must participate in training or inference.
The system may load official PDEBench checkpoints for finetuning when useful.
The system should distinguish method innovation from engineering tuning.
Evaluation should consider scientific performance, computational economy, and evolution-logic rigor.
The system should operate autonomously after receiving the research brief, with minimal manual intervention.
Experiments should preferentially use local GPUs 6 and 7, both A800 40GB.
All downloaded data, generated code, experiment outputs, logs, and reports must remain under /data0/ziyi/PDEFlow_Runs.
deliverables: Structured literature review notes and method taxonomy
Acquisition logs for datasets, repositories, checkpoints, and environments
Bottleneck analysis and candidate research directions
Executable child-program workspaces and experiment plans
Experiment logs, parsed metrics, and failure analysis
Stage reports and a final research report

secrets.purpose: OpenRouter API key for OpenAI-compatible model access through the Agents SDK.
Optional higher GitHub API rate limits for repository discovery.
